info.name: Scenario: Creating an Emergency Calling Service
info.description:
To do this you need to find DIDs that support emergency calling and are not yet assigned to any Emergency Calling Service. Check the Emergency Requirements for the country and DID Group Type to understand what identity and address details are required. Select or create an Identity and Address that meets the requirements, then create an Emergency Verification. A new Emergency Calling Service will be created automatically.

> ### Attention
>
> Requests should be run one-by-one on real environment for real values usage

Steps 4 and 6 are **optional** — run them only if you need to create a new Identity or Address. If you already have an existing Identity and Address, skip those steps.

#### Environment variables

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `host` | API host |
| `apiKey` | Your DIDWW API key |

#### Collection variables

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `did.id` | DID id available for emergency registration |
| `did_group.id` | DID Group id extracted from the selected DID |
| `country.id` | Country id extracted from the selected DID Group |
| `did_group_type.id` | DID Group Type id extracted from the selected DID Group |
| `emergency_requirement.id` | Emergency Requirement id for the selected country and DID Group Type |
| `identity.id` | Identity id to use for emergency verification |
| `address.id` | Address id linked to the selected identity |
| `emergency_verification.id` | Created Emergency Verification id |
| `emergency_calling_service.id` | Emergency Calling Service id created automatically |

exec: pm.test("DID available for Emergency Calling Service exist", function () {
// find first DID with no emergency calling service assigned
// set collection variable values if test successful
// if DID has identity assigned, use it; otherwise will be selected in Get Identities step
// extract country.id and did_group_type.id from included
const didGroup = response.included && response.included.find(i => i.type === "did_groups" && i.id === did.relationships.did_group.data.id);
// proceed next request in Collection Runner if test successful

request.description: Returns a list of DIDs owned by your account that support emergency calling and do not have it enabled yet. The response includes the related DID Group, Country, DID Group Type, Identity (if assigned), Emergency Calling Service (to check if already assigned), and Address Verification (if exists).

query.description:
`string` - Filters by the did_group.features field.
* **Allow blank** - *No*.
* **Allow array** - *Yes*.

`boolean` - Filters by the `emergency_enabled` field.
* **Allow blank** - *No*.
* **Allow array** - *No*.

response.name: 200 Ok (DID without Identity)
200 Ok (DID with Identity from End User Registration)
200 Ok (DID with Porting Identity)

exec: // set collection variable value if test successful
// proceed next request in Collection Runner if test successful

request.description: Returns the list of emergency requirements for the selected country and DID Group Type. Review the `requirement_restriction_message`, mandatory fields and `estimate_setup_time` to understand what identity and address details are required before proceeding.

query.description:
`string` - Filters by country id.
* **Allow blank** - *No*.
* **Allow array** - *No*.

`string` - Filters by DID group type id.
* **Allow blank** - *No*.
* **Allow array** - *No*.

response.name: 200 Ok (Filter by country.id and did_group_type.id)

exec: // set collection variable value if test successful

request.description: Returns a list of Identities owned by your account. Select the identity that matches the Emergency Requirement (identity type, mandatory fields). If the DID already has an assigned identity from step 1, that identity must be used.

response.name: 200 Ok (Simple request)

request.description: Optional step. Create a new Identity if you do not have one yet. Use the `id` from the response as `{{identity.id}}` in the next steps. Two response variants are provided: Personal Identity and Business Identity. Run the appropriate one depending on your use case. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **identities** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **identity_type** (*string, required*): **personal** or **business** > > > > Type of Identity. > > > > > > > **first_name** (*string, required*) > > > > First name (or company representative first name). > > > > > > > **last_name** (*string, required*) > > > > Last name (or company representative last name). > > > > > > > **phone_number** (*string, required*) > > > > Phone number. Only digits allowed. > > > > > > > **company_name** (*string, required for Business*) > > > > Company name. Required when `identity_type` is `Business`. > > > > > > > **company_reg_number** (*string, optional*) > > > > Company registration number. > > > > > > > **vat_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Company VAT ID. > > > > > > > **personal_tax_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Personal or company representative Tax ID. > > > > > > > **birth_date** (*DateTime[string], optional*) > > > > Birth date. > > > > > > > **id_number** (*string, optional*) > > > > ID number. > > > > > > > **description** (*string, optional*) > > > > Description. > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for identity in external system. > > > > > > > **contact_email** (*string, optional*) > > > > Contact email. > > > > > > `}` > > > > > **relationships** (*object, optional*) `{` > > > > > > > **country** (*object, required for Business, optional for Personal*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the country for identity. > > > > > > > **birth_country** (*object, optional*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the birth country for identity. > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}`

response.name: 201 Created (Create Personal Identity)
201 Created (Create Business Identity)

request.description: Returns a list of Addresses linked to the selected Identity. Select the address that meets the Emergency Requirement area level (City or Country).

query.description:
`string` - Filters by the id field of identity relationship.
* **Allow blank** - *No*.
* **Allow array** - *No*.

response.name: 200 Ok (Filter by identity.id)

request.description: Optional step. Create a new Address linked to the Identity if you do not have one yet. Use the `id` from the response as `{{address.id}}` in the next steps. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **addresses** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **city_name** (*string, required*) > > > > City name of the address. > > > > > > > **postal_code** (*string, required*) > > > > Postal code of the address. > > > > > > > **address** (*string, required*) > > > > Full address. > > > > > > > **description** (*string, optional*) > > > > The description of the address. > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for Address in external system. > > > > > > `}` > > > > > **relationships** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **identity** (*object, required*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the [*Identity*](#identity-object) for the address. > > > > > > > **country** (*object, required*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the [*Country*](#country-object) for the address. > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}`

exec: // proceed next request in Collection Runner if test successful

request.description: Checks if the [Address](#address-object) and or [Identity](#identity-object) created is valid against the [Emergency Requirement](#emergency-requirement-object). #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **emergency_requirement_validations** > > > > > **relationships** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **emergency_requirement** (*object, required*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the [*Emergency Requirement*](#emergency-requirement-object). > > > > > > > **address** (*object, optional*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the [*Address*](#address-object). > > > > > > > **identity** (*object, optional*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the [*Identity*](#identity-object). > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Request Body Examples ``` json { "data": { "type": "emergency_requirement_validations", "relationships": { "emergency_requirement": { "data": { "id": "d210e6e4-1716-4342-b04c-7a6ef6feb171", "type": "emergency_requirements" } }, "address": { "data": { "id": "49f09b7f-c5bf-4c90-bac1-a8b335d2c3f0", "type": "addresses" } }, "identity": { "data": { "id": "0c95a4c3-c5e6-4ea0-a8ba-bfb65850913c", "type": "identities" } } } } } ``` * * * ``` json { "data": { "type": "emergency_requirement_validations", "relationships": { "emergency_requirement": { "data": { "id": "d210e6e4-1716-4342-b04c-7a6ef6feb171", "type": "emergency_requirements" } } "identity": { "data": { "id": "0c95a4c3-c5e6-4ea0-a8ba-bfb65850913c", "type": "identities" } } } } } ``` * * * ``` json { "data": { "type": "emergency_requirement_validations", "relationships": { "emergency_requirement": { "data": { "id": "d210e6e4-1716-4342-b04c-7a6ef6feb171", "type": "emergency_requirements" } }, "address": { "data": { "id": "49f09b7f-c5bf-4c90-bac1-a8b335d2c3f0", "type": "addresses" } } } } } ```

exec: // set collection variable values if test successful
// proceed next request in Collection Runner if test successful

request.description: Creates an Emergency Verification in New Calling Service mode. Use this mode when registering emergency calling for DIDs for the first time. A new Emergency Calling Service will be created automatically. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **emergency_verifications** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, optional*) `{` > > > > > > > **callback_url** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTPS endpoint to where events related to verification status will be delivered. > > > > > > > **callback_method** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTP method used for verification status events. Allowed values: `get`, `post`. Required if `callback_url` is provided. > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for Emergency Verification in external system. > > > > > > `}` > > > > > **relationships** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **address** (*object, required*): [***to-one***](#updating-to-one-relationships) > > > > Specifies the ID of [*Address*](#address-object) for emergency verification. > > > > > > > **dids** (*object, required*): [***to-many***](#updating-to-many-relationships) > > > > Specifies the IDs of [*DIDs*](#did-object) to register for emergency calling. All DIDs must be from the same city or area. > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}` See [Callback details](#callbacks-details) for information about `callback_url` and `callback_method`. #### Request Body Example ``` json { "data": { "type": "emergency_verifications", "attributes": { "callback_url": "https://example.com/callbacks/emergency", "callback_method": "post" }, "relationships": { "address": { "data": { "id": "49f09b7f-c5bf-4c90-bac1-a8b335d2c3f0", "type": "addresses" } }, "dids": { "data": [ { "id": "f10d40c7-fd0b-4d63-bb9b-27810a1a8f5c", "type": "dids" }, { "id": "a2b3c4d5-e6f7-8901-abcd-ef1234567890", "type": "dids" } ] } } } } ``` > #### Note > > The `dids` relationship accepts one or more DID IDs. All DIDs must belong to the same city or area and must not be already assigned to another Emergency Calling Service. > If a DID has an assigned Identity, the same Identity must be used in the verification — otherwise a `422` error will be returned.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity (Identity Mismatch Error)

request.description: Returns a single Emergency Verification status and reason if the verification was rejected.

variable.description: Unique ID number for the emergency verification

exec: // prevent execution of next request in Collection Runner unless next request is defined.
pm.test("apiKey is set in Environment variables", ()=>{
pm.expect(apiKey, "apiKey is absent in Environment Variables").not.undefined
pm.expect(apiKey, "apiKey value is empty in Environment Variables").not.empty
pm.expect(apiKey, "apiKey value is not redefined in Environment Variables").not.eq("yourDidwwApiKey")
